---
title: Schedule
description: Schedule functions to run at specific times in the future
---

Schedule functions to run at specific times in the future using delayed calls. This is like setting an alarm clock for your code - the function will run after the specified delay, even if your service restarts.

## Delayed function calls

Delayed calls let you schedule a function to run at a specific time in the future. This is different from sleep - instead of pausing the current function, you're scheduling a completely separate function to run later.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processOrder = async (ctx: Context, order: Order) => {
  // Process the order immediately
  const result = await processOrderLogic(ctx, order);
  
  // Schedule a reminder email for tomorrow
  ctx.serviceCall(ReminderService).sendFollowUp(order.customerId, {
    delay: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 24 hours in milliseconds
  });
  
  // Schedule order cancellation if not paid within 15 minutes
  ctx.serviceCall(OrderService).cancelIfNotPaid(order.id, {
    delay: 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
  });
  
  return result;
};
```

```java Java
@Handler
public String processOrder(Context ctx, Order order) {
    // Process the order immediately
    String result = processOrderLogic(ctx, order);
    
    // Schedule a reminder email for tomorrow
    ReminderServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
        .sendFollowUp(order.getCustomerId())
        .delayedCall(Duration.ofDays(1));
    
    // Schedule order cancellation if not paid within 15 minutes
    OrderServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
        .cancelIfNotPaid(order.getId())
        .delayedCall(Duration.ofMinutes(15));
    
    return result;
}
```

```python Python
async def process_order(ctx: Context, order: Order):
    # Process the order immediately
    result = await process_order_logic(ctx, order)
    
    # Schedule a reminder email for tomorrow
    ReminderServiceClient.from_context(ctx).send_follow_up(
        order.customer_id
    ).delayed_call(timedelta(days=1))
    
    # Schedule order cancellation if not paid within 15 minutes
    OrderServiceClient.from_context(ctx).cancel_if_not_paid(
        order.id
    ).delayed_call(timedelta(minutes=15))
    
    return result
```
</CodeGroup>

The delay is durable - even if your service crashes and restarts, the scheduled call will still happen at the right time.

## Scheduling patterns

### Follow-up reminders

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const scheduleFollowUps = async (ctx: Context, customerId: string) => {
  // Schedule a series of follow-up emails
  
  // Day 1: Welcome tips
  ctx.serviceCall(EmailService).sendTips(customerId, {
    delay: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 1 day
  });
  
  // Day 7: Check-in email
  ctx.serviceCall(EmailService).sendCheckIn(customerId, {
    delay: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 7 days
  });
  
  // Day 30: Feedback survey
  ctx.serviceCall(EmailService).sendSurvey(customerId, {
    delay: 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 30 days
  });
  
  return { scheduled: true, customerId };
};
```
</CodeGroup>

### Timeout handlers

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const processWithTimeout = async (ctx: Context, taskId: string) => {
  // Start processing
  ctx.set("status", "processing");
  
  // Schedule timeout handler
  ctx.serviceCall(TimeoutService).handleTimeout(taskId, {
    delay: 30 * 60 * 1000 // 30 minutes
  });
  
  // Continue with actual processing...
  const result = await processTask(ctx, taskId);
  
  // Cancel timeout if we completed successfully
  ctx.serviceCall(TimeoutService).cancelTimeout(taskId);
  
  return result;
};
```
</CodeGroup>

### Maintenance tasks

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const scheduleMaintenanceTasks = async (ctx: Context) => {
  // Schedule daily cleanup at 2 AM
  const tomorrow = new Date();
  tomorrow.setDate(tomorrow.getDate() + 1);
  tomorrow.setHours(2, 0, 0, 0);
  const delay = tomorrow.getTime() - Date.now();
  
  ctx.serviceCall(MaintenanceService).runDailyCleanup({
    delay: delay
  });
  
  // Schedule weekly backup on Sundays at 3 AM
  const nextSunday = new Date();
  nextSunday.setDate(nextSunday.getDate() + (7 - nextSunday.getDay()));
  nextSunday.setHours(3, 0, 0, 0);
  const weeklyDelay = nextSunday.getTime() - Date.now();
  
  ctx.serviceCall(BackupService).runWeeklyBackup({
    delay: weeklyDelay
  });
  
  return { maintenanceScheduled: true };
};
```
</CodeGroup>

### Subscription renewals

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
export const scheduleRenewalReminders = async (ctx: Context, subscription: Subscription) => {
  const expirationDate = new Date(subscription.expiresAt);
  
  // Reminder 30 days before expiration
  const reminder30Days = new Date(expirationDate);
  reminder30Days.setDate(reminder30Days.getDate() - 30);
  const delay30 = reminder30Days.getTime() - Date.now();
  
  if (delay30 > 0) {
    ctx.serviceCall(NotificationService).sendRenewalReminder(subscription.userId, {
      delay: delay30,
      daysLeft: 30
    });
  }
  
  // Reminder 7 days before expiration
  const reminder7Days = new Date(expirationDate);
  reminder7Days.setDate(reminder7Days.getDate() - 7);
  const delay7 = reminder7Days.getTime() - Date.now();
  
  if (delay7 > 0) {
    ctx.serviceCall(NotificationService).sendRenewalReminder(subscription.userId, {
      delay: delay7,
      daysLeft: 7
    });
  }
  
  // Final reminder on expiration day
  const delayExpiration = expirationDate.getTime() - Date.now();
  
  if (delayExpiration > 0) {
    ctx.serviceCall(SubscriptionService).handleExpiration(subscription.id, {
      delay: delayExpiration
    });
  }
  
  return { remindersScheduled: true };
};
```
</CodeGroup>

## Scheduling different service types

### Schedule Basic Service calls

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Schedule stateless service function
ctx.serviceCall(DataProcessor).processDaily({
  delay: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000
});
```
</CodeGroup>

### Schedule Virtual Object calls

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Schedule Virtual Object function with specific key
ctx.objectCall(UserAccount, userId).sendBirthdayGreeting({
  delay: birthdayDelay
});
```
</CodeGroup>

### Schedule Workflow calls

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Schedule workflow to start
ctx.workflowCall(OrderWorkflow, orderId).processOrder(order, {
  delay: processingDelay
});

// Schedule signal to workflow
ctx.workflowSend(ApprovalWorkflow, requestId).escalate({
  delay: 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 2 hours
});
```
</CodeGroup>

## Calling scheduled functions from clients

You can also schedule function calls from client applications:

<CodeGroup>
```bash cURL
curl localhost:8080/ReminderService/sendFollowUp/send \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"customerId": "user-123"}' \
  --http1.1 \
  --raw '%3Cdelay%3E24h%3C/delay%3E'
```

```typescript TypeScript Client
// Schedule from client with 24 hour delay
restateClient
  .serviceClient(ReminderService)
  .sendFollowUp({ customerId: "user-123" }, { delay: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 });
```

```java Java Client
ReminderServiceClient.fromContext(ctx)
    .sendFollowUp("user-123")
    .delayedCall(Duration.ofDays(1));
```
</CodeGroup>

## Best practices

**Use meaningful delays**: Choose delays that make business sense and align with user expectations.

**Handle timezone considerations**: Be careful with absolute time calculations, especially across timezones.

**Consider cancellation**: For timeout scenarios, implement cancellation mechanisms when the main operation completes.

**Batch related schedules**: Group related scheduled operations to reduce complexity.

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript
// Good: Business-meaningful delays
ctx.serviceCall(ReminderService).sendFollowUp(userId, {
  delay: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 // 1 week follow-up
});

// Good: Implement cancellation for timeouts
ctx.serviceCall(TimeoutService).cancelTimeout(taskId);
```
</CodeGroup>

Scheduling enables sophisticated time-based coordination and business logic while maintaining reliability and exactly-once execution guarantees.
